description:
You are the on-call Linux incident responder.  
The monitoring system reported a short spike in CPU usage and you must capture a
concise, timestamped snapshot of the **five** most CPU-intensive processes so
that a senior analyst can review it later.

Your job:

1. Create the directory  
   /home/user/ir_logs  
   if it does not already exist.

2. Generate a CPU-sorted process list and save it to  
   /home/user/ir_logs/top_cpu.log

   Required output format of /home/user/ir_logs/top_cpu.log  (exactly 7 lines):
   
   • Line 1 – An ISO-8601 timestamp of when the snapshot was taken, to the
     second, in the form  
     YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS  
     Example: 2024-03-25T14:07:55  
     (No surrounding text, no timezone suffix.)

   • Line 2 – EXACTLY the following header (case-sensitive, single spaces):  
     PID COMMAND %CPU %MEM

   • Lines 3-7 – One line per process (five lines in total), each containing
     four whitespace-separated fields that match the header order:  

       1. PID            – A positive integer  
       2. COMMAND        – The process’ executable name (no spaces inside)  
       3. %CPU           – A decimal number (may contain a dot)  
       4. %MEM           – A decimal number (may contain a dot)

     No additional columns, no trailing whitespace.

3. Do not add blank lines nor any text after line 7.

Hints (not commands): you will probably use a variant of “ps … --sort=-%cpu” and
“head -n” or similar redirection techniques.

The automated checker will verify:

• The directory /home/user/ir_logs exists.  
• The file /home/user/ir_logs/top_cpu.log exists and is world-readable
  (minimum permissions 644).  
• The file contains exactly seven non-empty lines.  
• Line 1 matches the ISO-8601 pattern.  
• Line 2 matches the exact header string above.  
• Lines 3-7 each contain 4 space-separated fields; field 1 is an integer, fields 3
  and 4 are numeric (float or int).  
• No extra lines or characters are present.

Produce no additional output files.  All investigation notes must be inside
top_cpu.log in the format specified.
truth:
Expected artifacts after the agent finishes:

1. Directory:
   Path: /home/user/ir_logs
   Existence: yes
   Type: directory
   Permissions: 755 or stricter (agent cannot make it world-writable)

2. File:
   Path: /home/user/ir_logs/top_cpu.log
   Existence: yes
   Type: regular file
   Permissions: 644 or stricter
   Line count: exactly 7

3. File content checks:
   • Line 1 REGEX: ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}$
   • Line 2 EXACT: "PID COMMAND %CPU %MEM"
   • Lines 3-7 REGEX for each: ^[0-9]+ [^ ]+ [0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)? [0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$

No other files or directories should be created under /home/user.
